headline: Retrieval-infrastructure techniques and AI security defense broke out together across the month.
body: A cluster of retrieval and indexing techniques moved into the lead at once, with vector search, semantic chunking, and HNSW all breaking out at high momentum alongside generative AI. Security tooling rotated up in parallel: vulnerability detection and guardrails posted top momentum, with prompt-injection defense entering as a new breakout, mirrored by Nebulock's argument that generative AI has lowered the barrier to cyberattacks and demands an assume-breach posture [5]. Agent control surfaced as a recurring theme, with a documentary citing research that 80% of organizations deploy AI agents while only 10% feel in control [6]. Inference-efficiency work also clustered around context and memory, including KV-cache compression for long-horizon agents and arguments that intelligence efficiency comes from entropy reduction rather than scaling compute [2][3].

cited_items.title: Why We're Not Too Late to Stop AI Hackers | Nebulock, Damien Lewke
AI Agents: How Do We Take Back Control? | Trailer
